001package org.apache.juneau.commons.utils;
002
003import static java.util.stream.Collectors.*;
004import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
005import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Exceptions.*;
006import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ObjectUtils.*;
007
008import java.lang.reflect.*;
009import java.util.*;
010import java.util.function.*;
011import java.util.stream.*;
012
013import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
014

031
032/**
033 * Utility methods for working with collections and maps.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='section'>Complex Data Structure Construction:</h5>
036 * <p>
037 * This class provides convenient shorthand methods for creating complex nested data structures.
038 * The primary methods for building structures are:
039 * <ul>
040 *    <li>{@link #array(Object...)} - Creates arrays
041 *    <li>{@link #objectArray(Object...)} - Creates Object arrays (when type inference needs help)
042 *    <li>{@link #list(Object...)} / {@link #fixedSizeList(Object...)} - Creates modifiable lists
043 *    <li>{@link #map(Object, Object, Object, Object, Object, Object)} - Creates modifiable maps
044 *    <li>{@link #immutableMap(Object, Object, Object, Object, Object, Object)} - Creates unmodifiable maps
045 * </ul>
046 *
047 * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
048 * <p class='bjava'>
049 *    <jc>// Array of lists of maps</jc>
050 *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>data</jv> = <jsm>a</jsm>(
051 *       <jsm>l</jsm>(<jsm>m</jsm>(<js>"name"</js>, <js>"John"</js>, <js>"age"</js>, 30)),
052 *       <jsm>l</jsm>(<jsm>m</jsm>(<js>"name"</js>, <js>"Jane"</js>, <js>"age"</js>, 25))
053 *    );
054 *
055 *    <jc>// Compare with traditional Java syntax:</jc>
056 *    List&lt;Map&lt;String,Object&gt;&gt;[] <jv>data</jv> = <jk>new</jk> List[]{
057 *       <jk>new</jk> ArrayList&lt;&gt;(Arrays.asList(
058 *          <jk>new</jk> LinkedHashMap&lt;&gt;(){{
059 *             put(<js>"name"</js>, <js>"John"</js>);
060 *             put(<js>"age"</js>, 30);
061 *          }}
062 *       )),
063 *       <jk>new</jk> ArrayList&lt;&gt;(Arrays.asList(
064 *          <jk>new</jk> LinkedHashMap&lt;&gt;(){{
065 *             put(<js>"name"</js>, <js>"Jane"</js>);
066 *             put(<js>"age"</js>, 25);
067 *          }}
068 *       ))
069 *    };
070 * </p>
071 *
072 * <p class='bjava'>
073 *    <jc>// Complex nested structure: Array of lists containing maps and arrays</jc>
074 *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>complex</jv> = <jsm>a</jsm>(
075 *       <jsm>l</jsm>(
076 *          <jsm>m</jsm>(<js>"user"</js>, <js>"admin"</js>, <js>"roles"</js>, <jsm>a</jsm>(<js>"read"</js>, <js>"write"</js>, <js>"delete"</js>)),
077 *          <jsm>m</jsm>(<js>"user"</js>, <js>"guest"</js>, <js>"roles"</js>, <jsm>a</jsm>(<js>"read"</js>))
078 *       ),
079 *       <jsm>l</jsm>(
080 *          <jsm>m</jsm>(<js>"status"</js>, <js>"active"</js>, <js>"count"</js>, 42)
081 *       )
082 *    );
083 *
084 *    <jc>// Traditional Java equivalent (significantly more verbose):</jc>
085 *    List&lt;Map&lt;String,Object&gt;&gt;[] <jv>complex</jv> = <jk>new</jk> List[]{
086 *       <jk>new</jk> ArrayList&lt;&gt;(Arrays.asList(
087 *          <jk>new</jk> LinkedHashMap&lt;&gt;(){{
088 *             put(<js>"user"</js>, <js>"admin"</js>);
089 *             put(<js>"roles"</js>, <jk>new</jk> String[]{<js>"read"</js>, <js>"write"</js>, <js>"delete"</js>});
090 *          }},
091 *          <jk>new</jk> LinkedHashMap&lt;&gt;(){{
092 *             put(<js>"user"</js>, <js>"guest"</js>);
093 *             put(<js>"roles"</js>, <jk>new</jk> String[]{<js>"read"</js>});
094 *          }}
095 *       )),
096 *       <jk>new</jk> ArrayList&lt;&gt;(Arrays.asList(
097 *          <jk>new</jk> LinkedHashMap&lt;&gt;(){{
098 *             put(<js>"status"</js>, <js>"active"</js>);
099 *             put(<js>"count"</js>, 42);
100 *          }}
101 *       ))
102 *    };
103 * </p>
104 *
105 * <p class='bjava'>
106 *    <jc>// Using unmodifiable maps for immutable data</jc>
107 *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>config</jv> = <jsm>a</jsm>(
108 *       <jsm>m</jsm>(<js>"env"</js>, <js>"production"</js>, <js>"debug"</js>, <jk>false</jk>),
109 *       <jsm>m</jsm>(<js>"env"</js>, <js>"development"</js>, <js>"debug"</js>, <jk>true</jk>)
110 *    );
111 * </p>
112 *
113 * <h5 class='section'>Best Practices:</h5>
114 * <ul>
115 *    <li>Use {@link #array(Object...)} for arrays when type can be inferred, {@link #objectArray(Object...)} for Object arrays
116 *    <li>Use {@link #map(Object, Object, Object, Object, Object, Object)} when you need modifiable maps
117 *    <li>Use {@link #immutableMap(Object, Object, Object, Object, Object, Object)} when you need immutable/unmodifiable maps
118 *    <li>Use {@link #list(Object...)} or {@link #fixedSizeList(Object...)} for modifiable lists
119 *    <li>Static import these methods for maximum readability: <code>import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;</code>
120 * </ul>
121 *
122 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
123 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="../../../../../index.html#juneau-commons">juneau-commons</a>
124 * </ul>
125 */
126@SuppressWarnings({
127   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
128})
129public class CollectionUtils {
130
131   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
132   private static final String ARG_comparator = "comparator";
133   private static final String ARG_input = "input";
134   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
135   private static final String ARG_array = "array";
136   private static final String ARG_arrays = "arrays";
137   private static final String ARG_values = "values";
138   private static final String ARG_o = "o";
139
140   /**
141    * Traverses all elements in the specified objects and accumulates them into a list.
142    *
143    * <p>
144    * Input shapes are processed as follows:
145    * <ul>
146    *    <li><b>Flattened (recursively):</b> {@link Iterable}, {@link Stream}, and any array type (including all 8 primitive
147    *       array types via {@link ClassUtils#isArray}), including arbitrary nesting and mixing of these
148    *       (e.g. a {@code List<int[]>}, an array of {@code Stream}s, etc.).
149    *    <li><b>Leaf (added as-is):</b> anything else — {@link String}, {@link Map}, custom beans, boxed primitives, etc.
150    *    <li><b>{@code null} handling:</b> a {@code null} root, or {@code null} encountered mid-traversal, contributes nothing
151    *       to the result — not even a {@code null} element.
152    * </ul>
153    *
154    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
155    * <p class='bjava'>
156    *    List&lt;Object&gt; <jv>nested</jv> = List.of(<jk>new</jk> <jk>int</jk>[]{1, 2}, List.of(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>));
157    *
158    *    <jc>// Returns [1, 2, "a", "b"]</jc>
159    *    List&lt;Object&gt; <jv>result</jv> = <jsm>accumulate</jsm>(<jv>nested</jv>);
160    *
161    *    <jc>// Multi-root: returns [1, 2, "a", "b"]</jc>
162    *    List&lt;Object&gt; <jv>result2</jv> = <jsm>accumulate</jsm>(<jk>new</jk> <jk>int</jk>[]{1, 2}, <js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>);
163    * </p>
164    *
165    * @param <T> The element type.
166    * @param o The objects to traverse. Can be <jk>null</jk> or empty, in which case an empty list is returned.
167    * @return A new modifiable list containing all accumulated leaf elements.
168    */
169   @SuppressWarnings({
170      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
171   })
172   public static <T> List<T> accumulate(Object... o) {
173      var l = list();
174      if (o != null)
175         for (var x : o)
176            traverse(x, l::add);
177      return (List<T>)l;
178   }
179
180   /**
181    * Traverses all elements in the specified objects and returns them as a lazily-evaluated stream,
182    * without building an intermediate list.
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * Accepts the same input shapes as {@link #accumulate(Object...)} — see that method's Javadoc for the full
186    * list of flattened vs. leaf types and null-handling semantics.
187    *
188    * <p>
189    * Named {@code deepStream} rather than {@code stream} to avoid an erasure conflict with the existing
190    * shallow-array overload {@link #stream(Object[])}, which has the same erased parameter type ({@code Object[]}).
191    *
192    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
193    * <p class='bjava'>
194    *    List&lt;Object&gt; <jv>nested</jv> = List.of(<jk>new</jk> <jk>int</jk>[]{1, 2}, List.of(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>));
195    *
196    *    <jc>// Lazily yields 1, 2, "a", "b"</jc>
197    *    <jsm>deepStream</jsm>(<jv>nested</jv>).forEach(System.<jk>out</jk>::println);
198    * </p>
199    *
200    * @param <T> The element type.
201    * @param o The objects to traverse. Can be <jk>null</jk> or empty, in which case an empty stream is returned.
202    * @return A lazily-evaluated stream of all leaf elements.
203    */
204   public static <T> Stream<T> deepStream(Object... o) {
205      if (o == null)
206         return Stream.empty();
207      return Arrays.stream(o).flatMap(CollectionUtils::traverseToStream);
208   }
209
210   /**
211    * Adds all the specified values to the specified collection.
212    * Creates a new set if the value is <jk>null</jk>.
213    *
214    * @param <E> The element type.
215    * @param value The collection to add to.
216    * @param entries The entries to add.
217    * @return The set.
218    */
219   @SafeVarargs
220   public static <E> List<E> addAll(List<E> value, E...entries) {
221      if (isNotNull(entries)) {
222         if (value == null)
223            value = list(entries);
224         else
225            Collections.addAll(value, entries);
226      }
227      return value;
228   }
229
230   /**
231    * Adds all the specified values to the specified collection.
232    * Creates a new set if the value is <jk>null</jk>.
233    *
234    * @param <E> The element type.
235    * @param value The collection to add to.
236    * @param entries The entries to add.
237    * @return The set.
238    */
239   public static <E> List<E> addAll(List<E> value, List<E> entries) {
240      if (isNotNull(entries)) {
241         if (value == null)
242            value = copyOf(entries);
243         else
244            value.addAll(entries);
245      }
246      return value;
247   }
248
249   /**
250    * Adds all the specified values to the specified collection.
251    * Creates a new set if the value is <jk>null</jk>.
252    *
253    * @param <E> The element type.
254    * @param value The collection to add to.
255    * @param entries The entries to add.
256    * @return The set.
257    */
258   @SafeVarargs
259   public static <E> Set<E> addAll(Set<E> value, E...entries) {
260      if (isNotNull(entries)) {
261         if (value == null)
262            value = set(entries);
263         else
264            Collections.addAll(value, entries);
265      }
266      return value;
267   }
268
269   /**
270    * Adds all the specified values to the specified collection.
271    * Creates a new set if the value is <jk>null</jk>.
272    *
273    * @param <E> The element type.
274    * @param value The collection to add to.
275    * @param entries The entries to add.
276    * @return The set.
277    */
278   @SafeVarargs
279   public static <E> SortedSet<E> addAll(SortedSet<E> value, E...entries) {
280      if (isNotNull(entries)) {
281         if (value == null)
282            value = sortedSet(entries);
283         else
284            Collections.addAll(value, entries);
285      }
286      return value;
287   }
288
289   /**
290    * Appends one or more elements to an array.
291    *
292    * @param <T> The element type.
293    * @param array The array to append to.
294    * @param newElements The new elements to append to the array.
295    * @return A new array with the specified elements appended.
296    */
297   @SuppressWarnings({
298      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
299   })
300   public static <T> T[] addAll(T[] array, T...newElements) {
301      if (array == null)
302         return newElements;
303      if (newElements.length == 0)
304         return array;
305      var a = (T[])Array.newInstance(array.getClass().getComponentType(), array.length + newElements.length);
306      System.arraycopy(array, 0, a, 0, array.length);
307      System.arraycopy(newElements, 0, a, array.length, newElements.length);
308      return a;
309   }
310
311   /**
312    * Creates an array of the specified component type and length.
313    *
314    * @param <E> The component type of the array.
315    * @param componentType The component type of the array.
316    * @param length The length of the array.
317    * @return A new array of the specified type and length. Never <jk>null</jk>.
318    */
319   @SuppressWarnings({
320      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
321   })
322   public static <E> E[] array(Class<E> componentType, int length) {
323      return (E[])Array.newInstance(componentType, length);
324   }
325
326   /**
327    * Converts the specified collection to an array.
328    *
329    * @param <E> The element type.
330    * @param value The collection to convert.
331    * @param componentType The component type of the array.
332    * @return A new array.
333    */
334   @SuppressWarnings({
335      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
336   })
337   public static <E> E[] array(Collection<E> value, Class<E> componentType) {
338      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
339      var array = (E[])Array.newInstance(componentType, value.size());
340      return value.toArray(array);
341   }
342
343   /**
344    * Converts any array (including primitive arrays) to a List.
345    *
346    * @param array The array to convert. Can be any array type including primitives.
347    * @return A List containing the array elements. Primitive values are auto-boxed.
348    *         Returns null if the input is null.
349    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the input is not an array.
350    */
351   @SuppressWarnings({
352      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for array conversion
353   })
354   public static List<Object> arrayToList(Object array) {
355      assertArgNotNull(ARG_array, array);
356      assertArg(ClassUtils.isArray(array), "Input must be an array but was {0}", ClassUtils.className(array));
357
358      var componentType = array.getClass().getComponentType();
359      var length = Array.getLength(array);
360      var result = new ArrayList<>(length);
361
362      // Handle primitive arrays specifically for better performance
363      if (componentType.isPrimitive()) {
364         if (componentType == int.class) {
365            var arr = (int[])array;
366            for (var value : arr) {
367               result.add(value);
368            }
369         } else if (componentType == long.class) {
370            var arr = (long[])array;
371            for (var value : arr) {
372               result.add(value);
373            }
374         } else if (componentType == double.class) {
375            var arr = (double[])array;
376            for (var value : arr) {
377               result.add(value);
378            }
379         } else if (componentType == float.class) {
380            var arr = (float[])array;
381            for (var value : arr) {
382               result.add(value);
383            }
384         } else if (componentType == boolean.class) {
385            var arr = (boolean[])array;
386            for (var value : arr) {
387               result.add(value);
388            }
389         } else if (componentType == byte.class) {
390            var arr = (byte[])array;
391            for (var value : arr) {
392               result.add(value);
393            }
394         } else if (componentType == char.class) {
395            var arr = (char[])array;
396            for (var value : arr) {
397               result.add(value);
398            }
399         } else /* (componentType == short.class) */ {
400            var arr = (short[])array;
401            for (var value : arr) {
402               result.add(value);
403            }
404         }
405      } else {
406         // Handle Object arrays
407         for (var i = 0; i < length; i++) {
408            result.add(Array.get(array, i));
409         }
410      }
411
412      return result;
413   }
414
415   /**
416    * Combine an arbitrary number of arrays into a single array.
417    *
418    * @param <E> The element type.
419    * @param arrays Collection of arrays to combine.
420    * @return A new combined array, or an empty array if all arrays are <jk>null</jk>.
421    */
422   @SuppressWarnings({
423      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
424   })
425   public static <E> E[] combine(E[]...arrays) {
426      assertArgNotNull(ARG_arrays, arrays);
427      int l = 0;
428      E[] a1 = null;
429      for (var a : arrays) {
430         if (a1 == null && isNotNull(a))
431            a1 = a;
432         l += (a == null ? 0 : a.length);
433      }
434      if (a1 == null)
435         return (E[]) new Object[0];
436      var a = (E[])Array.newInstance(a1.getClass().getComponentType(), l);
437      int i = 0;
438      for (var aa : arrays)
439         if (isNotNull(aa))
440            for (var t : aa)
441               a[i++] = t;
442      return a;
443   }
444
445   /**
446    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified array contains the specified element using the {@link String#equals(Object)}
447    * method.
448    *
449    * @param element The element to check for.
450    * @param array The array to check.
451    * @return
452    *    <jk>true</jk> if the specified array contains the specified element,
453    *    <jk>false</jk> if the array or element is <jk>null</jk>.
454    */
455   public static <T> boolean contains(T element, T[] array) {
456      return indexOf(element, array) != -1;
457   }
458
459   /**
460    * Copies the specified array into the specified list.
461    *
462    * <p>
463    * Works on both object and primitive arrays.
464    *
465    * @param array The array to copy into a list.
466    * @param list The list to copy the values into.
467    * @return The same list passed in.
468    */
469   @SuppressWarnings({
470      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked cast from array
471      "rawtypes"   // Raw types necessary for generic array handling
472   })
473   public static List copyArrayToList(Object array, List list) {
474      if (isNotNull(array)) {
475         var length = Array.getLength(array);
476         for (var i = 0; i < length; i++)
477            list.add(Array.get(array, i));
478      }
479      return list;
480   }
481
482   /**
483    * Creates a new collection from the specified collection.
484    *
485    * @param <E> The element type.
486    * @param val The value to copy from.
487    * @return A new {@link LinkedHashSet}, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was null.
488    */
489   public static <E> Collection<E> copyOf(Collection<E> val) {
490      return val == null ? null : new LinkedHashSet<>(val);
491   }
492
493   /**
494    * Convenience method for copying a list.
495    *
496    * @param <E> The element type.
497    * @param value The list to copy.
498    * @return A new modifiable list.
499    */
500   public static <E> List<E> copyOf(List<E> value) {
501      return value == null ? null : new ArrayList<>(value);
502   }
503
504   /**
505    * Makes a deep copy of the specified list.
506    *
507    * @param l The list to copy.
508    * @param valueMapper The function to apply to each value in the list.
509    * @param <E> The entry type.
510    * @return A new list with the same values as the specified list, but with values transformed by the specified function.  Null if the list being copied was null.
511    */
512   public static <E> List<E> copyOf(List<E> l, Function<? super E,? extends E> valueMapper) {
513      return copyOf(l, valueMapper, LinkedList::new);
514   }
515
516   /**
517    * Makes a deep copy of the specified list.
518    *
519    * @param l The list to copy.
520    * @param valueMapper The function to apply to each value in the list.
521    * @param <E> The entry type.
522    * @param listFactory The factory for creating the list.
523    * @return A new list with the same values as the specified list, but with values transformed by the specified function.  Null if the list being copied was null.
524    */
525   public static <E> List<E> copyOf(List<E> l, Function<? super E,? extends E> valueMapper, Supplier<List<E>> listFactory) {
526      return l == null ? null : l.stream().map(valueMapper).collect(toCollection(listFactory));
527   }
528
529   /**
530    * Creates a new map from the specified map.
531    *
532    * @param <K> The key type.
533    * @param <V> The value type.
534    * @param val The value to copy from.
535    * @return A new {@link LinkedHashMap}, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was null.
536    */
537   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> copyOf(Map<K,V> val) {
538      return val == null ? null : new LinkedHashMap<>(val);
539   }
540
541   /**
542    * Makes a deep copy of the specified map.
543    *
544    * @param <K> The key type.
545    * @param <V> The value type.
546    * @param m The map to copy.
547    * @param valueMapper The function to apply to each value in the map.
548    * @return A new map with the same keys as the specified map, but with values transformed by the specified function.  Null if the map being copied was null.
549    */
550   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> copyOf(Map<K,V> m, Function<? super V,? extends V> valueMapper) {
551      return copyOf(m, valueMapper, LinkedHashMap::new);
552   }
553
554   /**
555    * Makes a deep copy of the specified map.
556    *
557    * @param <K> The key type.
558    * @param <V> The value type.
559    * @param m The map to copy.
560    * @param valueMapper The function to apply to each value in the map.
561    * @param mapFactory The factory for creating the map.
562    * @return A new map with the same keys as the specified map, but with values transformed by the specified function.  Null if the map being copied was null.
563    */
564   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> copyOf(Map<K,V> m, Function<? super V,? extends V> valueMapper, Supplier<Map<K,V>> mapFactory) {
565      return m == null ? null : m.entrySet().stream().collect(toMap(Map.Entry::getKey, e -> valueMapper.apply(e.getValue()), (a, b) -> b, mapFactory));
566   }
567
568   /**
569    * Creates a new set from the specified collection.
570    *
571    * @param <E> The element type.
572    * @param val The value to copy from.
573    * @return A new {@link LinkedHashSet}, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was null.
574    */
575   public static <E> Set<E> copyOf(Set<E> val) {
576      return val == null ? null : new LinkedHashSet<>(val);
577   }
578
579   /**
580    * Makes a deep copy of the specified list.
581    *
582    * @param l The list to copy.
583    * @param valueMapper The function to apply to each value in the list.
584    * @param <E> The entry type.
585    * @return A new list with the same values as the specified list, but with values transformed by the specified function.  Null if the list being copied was null.
586    */
587   public static <E> Set<E> copyOf(Set<E> l, Function<? super E,? extends E> valueMapper) {
588      return copyOf(l, valueMapper, LinkedHashSet::new);
589   }
590
591   /**
592    * Makes a deep copy of the specified list.
593    *
594    * @param l The list to copy.
595    * @param valueMapper The function to apply to each value in the list.
596    * @param <E> The entry type.
597    * @param setFactory The factory for creating sets.
598    * @return A new list with the same values as the specified list, but with values transformed by the specified function.  Null if the list being copied was null.
599    */
600   public static <E> Set<E> copyOf(Set<E> l, Function<? super E,? extends E> valueMapper, Supplier<Set<E>> setFactory) {
601      return l == null ? null : l.stream().map(valueMapper).collect(toCollection(setFactory));
602   }
603
604   /**
605    * Makes a copy of the specified array.
606    *
607    * @param array The array to copy.
608    * @param <T> The element type.
609    * @return A new copy of the array, or <jk>null</jk> if the array was <jk>null</jk>.s
610    */
611   public static <T> T[] copyOf(T[] array) {
612      return array == null ? null : Arrays.copyOf(array, array.length);
613   }
614
615   /**
616    * Returns the next value from an iterator, or an empty Optional if there are no more elements.
617    *
618    * <p>
619    * This is a null-safe operation. Returns an empty Optional if the iterator is <jk>null</jk> or
620    * has no more elements.
621    *
622    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
623    * <p class='bjava'>
624    *    Iterator&lt;String&gt; <jv>it</jv> = list.iterator();
625    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>first</jv> = <jsm>next</jsm>(<jv>it</jv>);  <jc>// Optional.of("first")</jc>
626    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>second</jv> = <jsm>next</jsm>(<jv>it</jv>);  <jc>// Optional.of("second")</jc>
627    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>empty</jv> = <jsm>next</jsm>(<jv>it</jv>);   <jc>// Optional.empty()</jc>
628    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>nullResult</jv> = <jsm>next</jsm>(<jk>null</jk>);  <jc>// Optional.empty()</jc>
629    * </p>
630    *
631    * @param <E> The element type.
632    * @param iterator The iterator. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
633    * @return An Optional containing the next element, or empty if the iterator is <jk>null</jk> or has no more elements.
634    */
635   public static <E> Optional<E> next(Iterator<? extends E> iterator) {
636      if (iterator == null || !iterator.hasNext())
637         return emptyOptional();
638      return optional(iterator.next());
639   }
640
641   /**
642    * Returns the first element in an iterable.
643    *
644    * <p>
645    * This is a null-safe operation. Returns an empty Optional if the iterable is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
646    *
647    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
648    * <p class='bjava'>
649    *    List&lt;String&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jsm>l</jsm>(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>"c"</js>);
650    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>first</jv> = <jsm>first</jsm>(<jv>list</jv>);  <jc>// Optional.of("a")</jc>
651    *
652    *    Set&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>set</jv> = <jsm>s</jsm>(1, 2, 3);
653    *    Optional&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>firstInt</jv> = <jsm>first</jsm>(<jv>set</jv>);  <jc>// Optional.of(1) or another element</jc>
654    *
655    *    EnumSet&lt;MyEnum&gt; <jv>enumSet</jv> = EnumSet.allOf(MyEnum.<jk>class</jk>);
656    *    Optional&lt;MyEnum&gt; <jv>firstEnum</jv> = <jsm>first</jsm>(<jv>enumSet</jv>);  <jc>// Optional.of(first enum value)</jc>
657    *
658    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>empty</jv> = <jsm>first</jsm>(Collections.emptyList());  <jc>// Optional.empty()</jc>
659    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>nullResult</jv> = <jsm>first</jsm>(<jk>null</jk>);  <jc>// Optional.empty()</jc>
660    * </p>
661    *
662    * @param <E> The element type.
663    * @param iterable The iterable. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
664    * @return An Optional containing the first element, or empty if the iterable is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
665    */
666   public static <E> Optional<E> first(Iterable<? extends E> iterable) {
667      if (iterable == null)
668         return emptyOptional();
669      Iterator<? extends E> iterator = iterable.iterator();
670      return next(iterator);
671   }
672
673   /**
674    * Iterates the specified array in reverse order.
675    *
676    * @param <E> The element type.
677    * @param value The array to iterate.
678    * @param action The action to perform.
679    */
680   public static <E> void forEachReverse(E[] value, Consumer<E> action) {
681      for (var i = value.length - 1; i >= 0; i--)
682         action.accept(value[i]);
683   }
684
685   /**
686    * Iterates the specified list in reverse order.
687    *
688    * @param <E> The element type.
689    * @param value The list to iterate.
690    * @param action The action to perform.
691    */
692   public static <E> void forEachReverse(List<E> value, Consumer<E> action) {
693      if (value instanceof ArrayList) {
694         for (var i = value.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--)
695            action.accept(value.get(i));
696      } else {
697         ListIterator<E> i = value.listIterator(value.size());
698         while (i.hasPrevious())
699            action.accept(i.previous());
700      }
701   }
702
703   /**
704    * Returns the index position of the element in the specified array using the {@link String#equals(Object)} method.
705    *
706    * @param element The element to check for.
707    * @param array The array to check.
708    * @return
709    *    The index position of the element in the specified array, or
710    *    <c>-1</c> if the array doesn't contain the element, or the array or element is <jk>null</jk>.
711    */
712   public static <T> int indexOf(T element, T[] array) {
713      if (element == null || array == null)
714         return -1;
715      for (var i = 0; i < array.length; i++)
716         if (equal(element, array[i]))
717            return i;
718      return -1;
719   }
720
721   /**
722    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified collection is null or empty.
723    *
724    * @param c The collection to check.
725    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified collection is null or empty.
726    */
727   public static boolean isEmpty(Collection<?> c) {
728      return c == null || c.isEmpty();
729   }
730
731   /**
732    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified map is null or empty.
733    *
734    * @param m The map to check.
735    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified map is null or empty.
736    */
737   public static boolean isEmpty(Map<?,?> m) {
738      return m == null || m.isEmpty();
739   }
740
741   /**
742    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified array is null or has a length of zero.
743    *
744    * @param array The array to check.
745    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified array is null or has a length of zero.
746    */
747   public static boolean isEmptyArray(Object[] array) {
748      return array == null || array.length == 0;
749   }
750
751   /**
752    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified array is not null and has a length greater than zero.
753    *
754    * @param array The array to check.
755    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified array is not null and has a length greater than zero.
756    */
757   public static boolean isNotEmptyArray(Object[] array) {
758      return isNotNull(array) && array.length > 0;
759   }
760
761   /**
762    * Creates a fixed-size list backed by the specified array (via {@link Arrays#asList(Object...)}).
763    *
764    * <p>
765    * The returned list is fixed-size: element values can be replaced via {@link List#set(int, Object)},
766    * but elements cannot be added or removed. Returns <jk>null</jk> if the input array is <jk>null</jk>.
767    * This differs from {@link #list(Object...)}, which returns a fully-modifiable {@link ArrayList}.
768    *
769    * @param <T> The element type.
770    * @param values The values to add to the list.
771    * @return A fixed-size list containing the specified values, or <jk>null</jk> if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
772    */
773   @SafeVarargs
774   public static <T> List<T> fixedSizeList(T...values) {
775      return values == null ? null : Arrays.asList(values);
776   }
777
778   /**
779    * Returns the last entry in an array.
780    *
781    * @param <E> The element type.
782    * @param l The array.
783    * @return The last element, or <jk>null</jk> if the array is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
784    */
785   public static <E> E last(E[] l) {
786      return (l == null || l.length == 0) ? null : l[l.length - 1];
787   }
788
789   /**
790    * Returns the last entry in a list.
791    *
792    * @param <E> The element type.
793    * @param l The list.
794    * @return The last element, or <jk>null</jk> if the list is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
795    */
796   public static <E> E last(List<E> l) {
797      return isEmpty(l) ? null : l.get(l.size() - 1);
798   }
799
800   /**
801    * Returns the length of the specified array.
802    *
803    * <p>
804    * This is a null-safe convenience method that wraps {@link Array#getLength(Object)}.
805    * Works with both object arrays and primitive arrays.
806    *
807    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
808    * <p class='bjava'>
809    *    String[] <jv>array1</jv> = {<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>};
810    *    <jk>int</jk>[] <jv>array2</jv> = {1, 2, 3};
811    *
812    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>len1</jv> = length(<jv>array1</jv>);  <jc>// Returns 2</jc>
813    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>len2</jv> = length(<jv>array2</jv>);  <jc>// Returns 3</jc>
814    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>len3</jv> = length(<jk>null</jk>);    <jc>// Returns 0</jc>
815    * </p>
816    *
817    * @param array The array object. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
818    * @return The length of the array, or <c>0</c> if the array is <jk>null</jk> or not an array.
819    */
820   public static int length(Object array) {
821      if (array == null)
822         return 0;
823      assertArg(array.getClass().isArray(), "Object is not an array");
824      return Array.getLength(array);
825   }
826
827   /**
828    * Shortcut for creating a modifiable list out of an array of values.
829    *
830    * @param <T> The element type.
831    * @param values The values to add to the list.
832    * @return A modifiable list containing the specified values, or <jk>null</jk> if the values array itself is <jk>null</jk>.
833    */
834   @SafeVarargs
835   public static <T> List<T> list(T...values) {
836      return values == null ? null : new ArrayList<>(fixedSizeList(values));
837   }
838
839   /**
840    * Convenience factory for a {@link Lists}.
841    *
842    * @param <E> The element type.
843    * @param type The element type.
844    * @return A new list builder.
845    */
846   public static <E> Lists<E> listBuilder(Class<E> type) {
847      return Lists.create(type);
848   }
849
850   /**
851    * Returns a null list.
852    *
853    * <p>Intentional null return for assertion testing.
854    *
855    * @param <T> The element type.
856    * @param type The element type class.
857    * @return <jk>null</jk>.
858    */
859   @SuppressWarnings({
860      "java:S1168",    // Intentional null return for assertion testing.
861      "java:S1172"     // Parameter type is used for type inference, not runtime behavior
862   })
863   public static <T> List<T> nullList(Class<T> type) {
864      return null;
865   }
866
867   /**
868    * Convenience method for creating an {@link ArrayList}.
869    *
870    * @param <E> The element type.
871    * @param elementType The element type.
872    * @param values The values to initialize the list with.
873    * @return A new modifiable list.
874    */
875   @SafeVarargs
876   public static <E> List<E> listOfType(Class<E> elementType, E...values) {
877      return list(values);
878   }
879
880   /**
881    * Convenience method for creating an {@link ArrayList} of the specified size.
882    *
883    * @param <E> The element type.
884    * @param size The initial size of the list.
885    * @return A new modifiable list.
886    */
887   public static <E> List<E> listOfSize(int size) {
888      return new ArrayList<>(size);
889   }
890
891   /**
892    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
893    *
894    * @param <K> The key type.
895    * @param <V> The value type.
896    * @return A new unmodifiable empty map.
897    */
898   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap() {
899      return Map.of();
900   }
901
902   /**
903    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
904    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
905    *
906    * @param <K> The key type.
907    * @param <V> The value type.
908    * @param k1 Key 1.
909    * @param v1 Value 1.
910    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
911    */
912   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1) {
913      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1), array(v1));
914   }
915
916   /**
917    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
918    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
919    *
920    * @param <K> The key type.
921    * @param <V> The value type.
922    * @param k1 Key 1.
923    * @param v1 Value 1.
924    * @param k2 Key 2.
925    * @param v2 Value 2.
926    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
927    */
928   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2) {
929      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1, k2), array(v1, v2));
930   }
931
932   /**
933    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
934    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
935    *
936    * @param <K> The key type.
937    * @param <V> The value type.
938    * @param k1 Key 1.
939    * @param v1 Value 1.
940    * @param k2 Key 2.
941    * @param v2 Value 2.
942    * @param k3 Key 3.
943    * @param v3 Value 3.
944    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
945    */
946   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3) {
947      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1, k2, k3), array(v1, v2, v3));
948   }
949
950   /**
951    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
952    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
953    *
954    * @param <K> The key type.
955    * @param <V> The value type.
956    * @param k1 Key 1.
957    * @param v1 Value 1.
958    * @param k2 Key 2.
959    * @param v2 Value 2.
960    * @param k3 Key 3.
961    * @param v3 Value 3.
962    * @param k4 Key 4.
963    * @param v4 Value 4.
964    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
965    */
966   @SuppressWarnings({
967      "java:S107" // Many parameters acceptable for convenience method
968   })
969   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4) {
970      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1, k2, k3, k4), array(v1, v2, v3, v4));
971   }
972
973   /**
974    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
975    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
976    *
977    * @param <K> The key type.
978    * @param <V> The value type.
979    * @param k1 Key 1.
980    * @param v1 Value 1.
981    * @param k2 Key 2.
982    * @param v2 Value 2.
983    * @param k3 Key 3.
984    * @param v3 Value 3.
985    * @param k4 Key 4.
986    * @param v4 Value 4.
987    * @param k5 Key 5.
988    * @param v5 Value 5.
989    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
990    */
991   @SuppressWarnings({
992      "java:S107" // Many parameters acceptable for convenience method
993   })
994   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5) {
995      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1, k2, k3, k4, k5), array(v1, v2, v3, v4, v5));
996   }
997
998   /**
999    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
1000    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
1001    *
1002    * @param <K> The key type.
1003    * @param <V> The value type.
1004    * @param k1 Key 1.
1005    * @param v1 Value 1.
1006    * @param k2 Key 2.
1007    * @param v2 Value 2.
1008    * @param k3 Key 3.
1009    * @param v3 Value 3.
1010    * @param k4 Key 4.
1011    * @param v4 Value 4.
1012    * @param k5 Key 5.
1013    * @param v5 Value 5.
1014    * @param k6 Key 6.
1015    * @param v6 Value 6.
1016    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
1017    */
1018   @SuppressWarnings({
1019      "java:S107" // Many parameters acceptable for convenience method
1020   })
1021   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6) {
1022      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1, k2, k3, k4, k5, k6), array(v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6));
1023   }
1024
1025   /**
1026    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
1027    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
1028    *
1029    * @param <K> The key type.
1030    * @param <V> The value type.
1031    * @param k1 Key 1.
1032    * @param v1 Value 1.
1033    * @param k2 Key 2.
1034    * @param v2 Value 2.
1035    * @param k3 Key 3.
1036    * @param v3 Value 3.
1037    * @param k4 Key 4.
1038    * @param v4 Value 4.
1039    * @param k5 Key 5.
1040    * @param v5 Value 5.
1041    * @param k6 Key 6.
1042    * @param v6 Value 6.
1043    * @param k7 Key 7.
1044    * @param v7 Value 7.
1045    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
1046    */
1047   @SuppressWarnings({
1048      "java:S107" // Many parameters acceptable for convenience method
1049   })
1050   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7) {
1051      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1, k2, k3, k4, k5, k6, k7), array(v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6, v7));
1052   }
1053
1054   /**
1055    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
1056    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
1057    *
1058    * @param <K> The key type.
1059    * @param <V> The value type.
1060    * @param k1 Key 1.
1061    * @param v1 Value 1.
1062    * @param k2 Key 2.
1063    * @param v2 Value 2.
1064    * @param k3 Key 3.
1065    * @param v3 Value 3.
1066    * @param k4 Key 4.
1067    * @param v4 Value 4.
1068    * @param k5 Key 5.
1069    * @param v5 Value 5.
1070    * @param k6 Key 6.
1071    * @param v6 Value 6.
1072    * @param k7 Key 7.
1073    * @param v7 Value 7.
1074    * @param k8 Key 8.
1075    * @param v8 Value 8.
1076    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
1077    */
1078   @SuppressWarnings({
1079      "java:S107" // Many parameters acceptable for convenience method
1080   })
1081   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8) {
1082      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1, k2, k3, k4, k5, k6, k7, k8), array(v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6, v7, v8));
1083   }
1084
1085   /**
1086    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
1087    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
1088    *
1089    * @param <K> The key type.
1090    * @param <V> The value type.
1091    * @param k1 Key 1.
1092    * @param v1 Value 1.
1093    * @param k2 Key 2.
1094    * @param v2 Value 2.
1095    * @param k3 Key 3.
1096    * @param v3 Value 3.
1097    * @param k4 Key 4.
1098    * @param v4 Value 4.
1099    * @param k5 Key 5.
1100    * @param v5 Value 5.
1101    * @param k6 Key 6.
1102    * @param v6 Value 6.
1103    * @param k7 Key 7.
1104    * @param v7 Value 7.
1105    * @param k8 Key 8.
1106    * @param v8 Value 8.
1107    * @param k9 Key 9.
1108    * @param v9 Value 9.
1109    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
1110    */
1111   @SuppressWarnings({
1112      "java:S107" // Many parameters acceptable for convenience method
1113   })
1114   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8, K k9, V v9) {
1115      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1, k2, k3, k4, k5, k6, k7, k8, k9), array(v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6, v7, v8, v9));
1116   }
1117
1118   /**
1119    * Convenience method for creating an unmodifiable map.
1120    * Unlike Map.of(...), supports null keys/values and preserves insertion order.
1121    *
1122    * @param <K> The key type.
1123    * @param <V> The value type.
1124    * @param k1 Key 1.
1125    * @param v1 Value 1.
1126    * @param k2 Key 2.
1127    * @param v2 Value 2.
1128    * @param k3 Key 3.
1129    * @param v3 Value 3.
1130    * @param k4 Key 4.
1131    * @param v4 Value 4.
1132    * @param k5 Key 5.
1133    * @param v5 Value 5.
1134    * @param k6 Key 6.
1135    * @param v6 Value 6.
1136    * @param k7 Key 7.
1137    * @param v7 Value 7.
1138    * @param k8 Key 8.
1139    * @param v8 Value 8.
1140    * @param k9 Key 9.
1141    * @param v9 Value 9.
1142    * @param k10 Key 10.
1143    * @param v10 Value 10.
1144    * @return A new unmodifiable map.
1145    */
1146   @SuppressWarnings({
1147      "java:S107" // Many parameters acceptable for convenience method
1148   })
1149   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> immutableMap(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8, K k9, V v9, K k10, V v10) {
1150      return new SimpleMap<>(array(k1, k2, k3, k4, k5, k6, k7, k8, k9, k10), array(v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6, v7, v8, v9, v10));
1151   }
1152
1153   /**
1154    * Convenience method for creating a {@link LinkedHashMap}.
1155    *
1156    * @param <K> The key type.
1157    * @param <V> The value type.
1158    * @return A new modifiable map.
1159    */
1160   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> map() {
1161      return new LinkedHashMap<>();
1162   }
1163
1164   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1165   // Array utilities
1166   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1167
1168   /**
1169    * Convenience method for creating a {@link LinkedHashMap}.
1170    *
1171    * @param <K> The key type.
1172    * @param <V> The value type.
1173    * @param k1 Key 1.
1174    * @param v1 Value 1.
1175    * @return A new modifiable map.
1176    */
1177   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> map(K k1, V v1) {
1178      var m = new LinkedHashMap<K,V>();
1179      m.put(k1, v1);
1180      return m;
1181   }

1338      m.put(k1, v1);
1339      m.put(k2, v2);
1340      m.put(k3, v3);
1341      m.put(k4, v4);
1342      m.put(k5, v5);
1343      m.put(k6, v6);
1344      m.put(k7, v7);
1345      return m;

1398    * @param k4 Key 4.
1399    * @param v4 Value 4.

1401    * @param v5 Value 5.
1402    * @param k6 Key 6.
1403    * @param v6 Value 6.
1404    * @param k7 Key 7.
1405    * @param v7 Value 7.
1406    * @param k8 Key 8.
1407    * @param v8 Value 8.
1408    * @param k9 Key 9.
1409    * @param v9 Value 9.
1410    * @return A new modifiable map.
1411    */
1412   @SuppressWarnings({
1413      "java:S107" // Many parameters acceptable for convenience method
1414   })
1415   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> map(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8, K k9, V v9) {
1416      var m = new LinkedHashMap<K,V>();
1417      m.put(k1, v1);
1418      m.put(k2, v2);
1419      m.put(k3, v3);
1420      m.put(k4, v4);
1421      m.put(k5, v5);
1422      m.put(k6, v6);
1423      m.put(k7, v7);
1424      m.put(k8, v8);
1425      m.put(k9, v9);
1426      return m;
1427   }

1429   /**

1431    *
