1433    * @param <V> The value type.
1434    * @param k1 Key 1.
1435    * @param v1 Value 1.
1436    * @param k2 Key 2.
1437    * @param v2 Value 2.
1438    * @param k3 Key 3.
1439    * @param v3 Value 3.
1440    * @param k4 Key 4.
1441    * @param v4 Value 4.
1442    * @param k5 Key 5.
1443    * @param v5 Value 5.
1444    * @param k6 Key 6.
1445    * @param v6 Value 6.
1446    * @param k7 Key 7.
1447    * @param v7 Value 7.
1448    * @param k8 Key 8.
1449    * @param v8 Value 8.
1450    * @param k9 Key 9.
1451    * @param v9 Value 9.
1452    * @param k10 Key 10.
1453    * @param v10 Value 10.
1454    * @return A new modifiable map.
1455    */
1456   @SuppressWarnings({
1457      "java:S107" // Many parameters acceptable for convenience method
1458   })
1459   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> map(K k1, V v1, K k2, V v2, K k3, V v3, K k4, V v4, K k5, V v5, K k6, V v6, K k7, V v7, K k8, V v8, K k9, V v9, K k10, V v10) {
1460      var m = new LinkedHashMap<K,V>();
1461      m.put(k1, v1);
1462      m.put(k2, v2);
1463      m.put(k3, v3);
1464      m.put(k4, v4);
1465      m.put(k5, v5);
1466      m.put(k6, v6);
1467      m.put(k7, v7);
1468      m.put(k8, v8);
1469      m.put(k9, v9);
1470      m.put(k10, v10);
1471      return m;
1472   }
1473
1474   /**
1475    * Convenience factory for a {@link Maps} builder.
1476    *
1477    * <p>
1478    * This is a shortcut for <c>Maps.create().ordered()</c>.
1479    *
1480    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1481    * <p class='bjava'>
1482    *    Map&lt;String,Object&gt; <jv>map</jv> = mapBuilder()
1483    *       .add(<js>"foo"</js>, 1)
1484    *       .add(<js>"bar"</js>, 2)
1485    *       .build();
1486    * </p>
1487    *
1488    * @param <K> The key type.
1489    * @param <V> The value type.
1490    * @return A new map builder.
1491    * @see Maps
1492    */
1493   @SuppressWarnings({
1494      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
1495   })
1496   public static <K,V> Maps<K,V> mapBuilder() {
1497      return (Maps<K,V>)Maps.create().ordered();
1498   }
1499
1500   /**
1501    * Convenience factory for a {@link Maps} with {@link String} keys and {@link Object} values.
1502    *
1503    * <p>
1504    * This is a shortcut for <c>Maps.create(String.<jk>class</jk>, Object.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
1505    *
1506    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1507    * <p class='bjava'>
1508    *    Map&lt;String,Object&gt; <jv>map</jv> = mapBuilder()
1509    *       .add(<js>"foo"</js>, 1)
1510    *       .add(<js>"bar"</js>, 2)
1511    *       .build();
1512    * </p>
1513    *
1514    * <p>
1515    * This builder supports optional filtering to automatically exclude unwanted values:
1516    * <p class='bjava'>
1517    *    <jc>// Build a map excluding null and empty values</jc>
1518    *    Map&lt;String,Object&gt; <jv>map</jv> = mapBuilder().filtered()
1519    *       .add(<js>"foo"</js>, <jk>null</jk>)       <jc>// Excluded</jc>
1520    *       .add(<js>"bar"</js>, <js>""</js>)         <jc>// Excluded</jc>
1521    *       .add(<js>"baz"</js>, <js>"value"</js>)    <jc>// Included</jc>
1522    *       .build();
1523    * </p>
1524    *
1525    * @return A new map builder.
1526    * @see Maps
1527    */
1528   @SuppressWarnings({
1529      "java:S100" // Method name uses underscore convention
1530   })
1531   public static Maps<String,Object> mapb_so() {
1532      return Maps.create(String.class, Object.class).ordered();
1533   }
1534
1535   /**
1536    * Convenience factory for a filtered, sorted, fluent map with {@link String} keys and {@link Object} values.
1537    *
1538    * <p>
1539    * This is a shortcut for <c>Maps.create(String.<jk>class</jk>, Object.<jk>class</jk>).filtered().sorted().buildFluent()</c>.
1540    *
1541    * <p>
1542    * This is typically used for creating property maps in <c>toString()</c> methods that need to be sorted and filtered.
1543    *
1544    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1545    * <p class='bjava'>
1546    *    <jc>// In a properties() method</jc>
1547    *    <jk>return</jk> filteredBeanPropertyMap()
1548    *       .a(<js>"name"</js>, <jv>name</jv>)
1549    *       .a(<js>"value"</js>, <jv>value</jv>);
1550    * </p>
1551    *
1552    * @return A new filtered, sorted, fluent map builder.
1553    * @see FluentMap
1554    * @see Maps
1555    */
1556   public static FluentMap<String,Object> filteredBeanPropertyMap() {
1557      return Maps.create(String.class, Object.class).filtered().sorted().buildFluent();
1558   }
1559
1560   /**
1561    * Convenience factory for a {@link Maps}.
1562    *
1563    * @param <K> The key type.
1564    * @param <V> The value type.
1565    * @param keyType The key type.
1566    * @param valueType The value type.
1567    * @return A new map builder.
1568    */
1569   public static <K,V> Maps<K,V> mapBuilder(Class<K> keyType, Class<V> valueType) {
1570      return Maps.create(keyType, valueType).ordered();
1571   }
1572
1573   /**
1574    * Returns a null map.
1575    *
1576    * <p>Intentional null return for assertion testing.
1577    *
1578    * @param <K> The key type.
1579    * @param <V> The value type.
1580    * @param keyType The key type class.
1581    * @param valueType The value type class.
1582    * @return <jk>null</jk>.
1583    */
1584   @SuppressWarnings({
1585      "java:S1168",    // Intentional null return for assertion testing. Consider Optional.
1586      "java:S1172"    // Parameters required for type inference in public API
1587   })
1588   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> nullMap(Class<K> keyType, Class<V> valueType) {
1589      return null;
1590   }
1591
1592   /**
1593    * Convenience method for creating a {@link LinkedHashMap}.
1594    *
1595    * @param <K> The key type.
1596    * @param <V> The value type.
1597    * @param keyType The key type.
1598    * @param valueType The value type.
1599    * @return A new modifiable map.
1600    */
1601   @SuppressWarnings({
1602      "java:S1172" // Parameters required for type inference in public API
1603   })
1604   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> mapOfType(Class<K> keyType, Class<V> valueType) {
1605      return map();
1606   }
1607
1608   /**
1609    * Adds all the specified values to the specified collection.
1610    * Creates a new set if the value is <jk>null</jk>.
1611    *
1612    * @param <E> The element type.
1613    * @param value The collection to add to.
1614    * @param entries The entries to add.
1615    * @return The set.
1616    */
1617   @SafeVarargs
1618   public static <E> List<E> prependAll(List<E> value, E...entries) {
1619      if (isNotNull(entries)) {
1620         if (value == null)
1621            value = list(entries);
1622         else
1623            value.addAll(0, fixedSizeList(entries));
1624      }
1625      return value;
1626   }
1627
1628   /**
1629    * Reverses the elements in the specified array in-place.
1630    *
1631    * @param <E> The element type.
1632    * @param array The array to reverse.
1633    * @return The same array, reversed.
1634    */
1635   public static <E> E[] reverse(E[] array) {
1636      for (var i = 0; i < array.length / 2; i++) {
1637         E temp = array[i];
1638         array[i] = array[array.length - i - 1];
1639         array[array.length - i - 1] = temp;
1640      }
1641      return array;
1642   }
1643
1644   /**
1645    * Returns a reversed view of the specified list.
1646    *
1647    * <p>
1648    * The returned list is a live view that reflects changes to the original list.
1649    *
1650    * @param <E> The element type.
1651    * @param list The list to reverse.
1652    * @return A reversed view of the list.
1653    */
1654   public static <E> List<E> reverse(List<E> list) {
1655      return new ReversedList<>(list);
1656   }
1657
1658   /**
1659    * Returns a reverse stream of the specified list.
1660    *
1661    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1662    * <p class='bjava'>
1663    *    List&lt;String&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jsm>list</jsm>(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>"c"</js>);
1664    *    Stream&lt;String&gt; <jv>reversed</jv> = <jsm>rstream</jsm>(<jv>list</jv>);
1665    *    <jc>// Produces stream: "c", "b", "a"</jc>
1666    * </p>
1667    *
1668    * @param <T> The element type.
1669    * @param value The list to stream in reverse order. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1670    * @return A stream of the list elements in reverse order, or an empty stream if the list is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1671    */
1672   public static <T> Stream<T> rstream(List<T> value) {
1673      if (value == null || value.isEmpty())
1674         return Stream.empty();
1675      return IntStream.range(0, value.size()).mapToObj(i -> value.get(value.size() - 1 - i));
1676   }
1677
1678   /**
1679    * Removes negation tokens from a list.
1680    *
1681    * <p>
1682    * A negation token is a string starting with <js>'-'</js> followed by at least one character.
1683    * When encountered, it removes the first prior occurrence of the corresponding positive token.
1684    *
1685    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1686    * <p class='bjava'>
1687    *    List&lt;String&gt; <jv>result</jv> = <jsm>removeNegations</jsm>(list(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>"-a"</js>, <js>"c"</js>));
1688    *    <jc>// Produces: ["b", "c"]</jc>
1689    * </p>
1690    *
1691    * @param input The input list. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1692    * @return The list with negation tokens applied, or the original list if no negation tokens were present.
1693    */
1694   public static List<String> removeNegations(List<String> input) {
1695      assertArgNotNull(ARG_input, input);
1696      var hasNegation = false;
1697      for (var token : input) {
1698         if (token != null && token.length() > 1 && token.charAt(0) == '-') {
1699            hasNegation = true;
1700            break;
1701         }
1702      }
1703      if (!hasNegation)
1704         return input;
1705      var out = new ArrayList<String>(input.size());
1706      for (var token : input) {
1707         if (token != null && token.length() > 1 && token.charAt(0) == '-')
1708            out.remove(token.substring(1));
1709         else
1710            out.add(token);
1711      }
1712      return out;
1713   }
1714
1715   /**
1716    * Creates a {@link TreeSet} with a custom comparator from a collection of elements.
1717    *
1718    * <p>
1719    * Null elements in the collection are silently skipped.
1720    *
1721    * @param <E> The element type.
1722    * @param comparator The comparator to use for ordering. Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1723    * @param elements The initial elements. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as empty).
1724    * @return A new {@link TreeSet} containing all non-null elements from the collection.
1725    */
1726   public static <E> SortedSet<E> treeSet(Comparator<? super E> comparator, Collection<? extends E> elements) {
1727      assertArgNotNull(ARG_comparator, comparator);
1728      var s = new TreeSet<E>(comparator);
1729      if (elements != null)
1730         for (var e : elements)
1731            if (e != null)
1732               s.add(e);
1733      return s;
1734   }
1735
1736   /**
1737    * Creates a case-insensitive {@link TreeSet} from a collection of strings.
1738    *
1739    * <p>
1740    * Equivalent to <c>treeSet(String.CASE_INSENSITIVE_ORDER, elements)</c>.
1741    * Null elements in the collection are silently skipped.
1742    *
1743    * @param elements The initial elements. Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as empty).
1744    * @return A new case-insensitive {@link TreeSet} containing all non-null elements from the collection.
1745    */
1746   public static SortedSet<String> treeSetCi(Collection<String> elements) {
1747      return treeSet(String.CASE_INSENSITIVE_ORDER, elements);
1748   }
1749
1750   /**
1751    * Convenience factory for a {@link Sets}.
1752    *
1753    * @param <E> The element type.
1754    * @param type The element type.
1755    * @return A new set builder.
1756    */
1757   public static <E> Sets<E> setBuilder(Class<E> type) {
1758      return Sets.create(type).ordered();
1759   }
1760
1761   /**
1762    * Convenience method for creating a {@link LinkedHashSet}.
1763    *
1764    * @param <E> The element type.
1765    * @param elementType The element type.
1766    * @param values The values to initialize the set with.
1767    * @return A new modifiable set.
1768    */
1769   @SafeVarargs
1770   public static <E> Set<E> setOfType(Class<E> elementType, E...values) {
1771      return set(values);
1772   }
1773
1774   /**
1775    * Convenience method for creating an {@link ArrayList} and sorting it.
1776    *
1777    * @param <E> The element type.
1778    * @param comparator The comparator to use to sort the list.
1779    * @param value The values to initialize the list with.
1780    * @return A new modifiable list.
1781    */
1782   public static <E> List<E> sortedList(Comparator<E> comparator, Collection<E> value) {
1783      List<E> l = toList(value);
1784      Collections.sort(l, comparator);
1785      return l;
1786   }
1787
1788   /**
1789    * Convenience method for creating an {@link ArrayList} and sorting it.
1790    *
1791    * @param <E> The element type.
1792    * @param comparator The comparator to use to sort the list.
1793    * @param values The values to initialize the list with.
1794    * @return A new modifiable list.
1795    */
1796   public static <E> List<E> sortedList(Comparator<E> comparator, E[] values) {
1797      List<E> l = list(values);
1798      Collections.sort(l, comparator);
1799      return l;
1800   }
1801
1802   /**
1803    * Convenience method for creating an {@link ArrayList} and sorting it.
1804    *
1805    * @param <E> The element type.
1806    * @param values The values to initialize the list with.
1807    * @return A new modifiable list.
1808    */
1809   @SuppressWarnings({
1810      "rawtypes",  // Raw types necessary for varargs handling
1811      "unchecked"  // Type erasure requires unchecked operations
1812   })
1813   @SafeVarargs
1814   public static <E> List<E> sortedList(E...values) {
1815      List<E> l = list(values);
1816      Collections.sort((List<Comparable>)l);
1817      return l;
1818   }
1819
1820   /**
1821    * Convenience method for creating a {@link TreeMap}.
1822    *
1823    * @param <K> The key type.
1824    * @param <V> The value type.
1825    * @return A new modifiable set.
1826    */
1827   public static <K,V> SortedMap<K,V> sortedMap() {
1828      return new TreeMap<>();
1829   }
1830
1831   /**
1832    * Convenience method for creating a {@link TreeSet}.
1833    *
1834    * <p>
1835    * Note: <jk>null</jk> values in the varargs array are automatically filtered out
1836    * because {@link TreeSet} does not support <jk>null</jk> elements.
1837    *
1838    * @param <E> The element type.
1839    * @param values The values to initialize the set with.
1840    *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (they will be filtered out).
1841    * @return A new modifiable set.
1842    */
1843   @SafeVarargs
1844   public static <E> SortedSet<E> sortedSet(E...values) {
1845      assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values);
1846      var l = new TreeSet<E>();
1847      for (var v : values)
1848         if (v != null)
1849            l.add(v);
1850      return l;
1851   }
1852
1853   /**
1854    * Returns a stream of the specified array.
1855    *
1856    * <p>
1857    * Gracefully handles <jk>null</jk> arrays by returning an empty stream.
1858    *
1859    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1860    * <p class='bjava'>
1861    *    String[] <jv>array</jv> = {<js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>"c"</js>};
1862    *
1863    *    <jc>// Prints "a", "b", "c"</jc>
1864    *    <jsm>stream</jsm>(<jv>array</jv>).forEach(System.<jk>out</jk>::println);
1865    *
1866    *    <jc>// Handles null gracefully - returns empty stream</jc>
1867    *    <jsm>stream</jsm>(<jk>null</jk>).forEach(System.<jk>out</jk>::println);  <jc>// Prints nothing</jc>
1868    * </p>
1869    *
1870    * @param <T> The element type.
1871    * @param array The array to stream. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
1872    * @return A stream of the array elements, or an empty stream if the array is <jk>null</jk>.
1873    */
1874   public static <T> Stream<T> stream(T[] array) {
1875      return array == null ? Stream.empty() : Arrays.stream(array);
1876   }
1877
1878   /**
1879    * Wraps the specified list in {@link Collections#unmodifiableList(List)}.
1880    *
1881    * @param <E> The element type.
1882    * @param value The list to wrap.
1883    * @return The wrapped list.
1884    */
1885   @SuppressWarnings({
1886      "java:S1168"     // Pass-through null by design. Consider empty list.
1887   })
1888   public static <E> List<E> synced(List<E> value) {
1889      return value == null ? null : Collections.synchronizedList(value);
1890   }
1891
1892   /**
1893    * Wraps the specified map in {@link Collections#unmodifiableMap(Map)}.
1894    *
1895    * @param <K> The key type.
1896    * @param <V> The value type.
1897    * @param value The map to wrap.
1898    * @return The wrapped map.
1899    */
1900   @SuppressWarnings({
1901      "java:S1168"     // Pass-through null by design. Consider empty map.
1902   })
1903   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> synced(Map<K,V> value) {
1904      return value == null ? null : Collections.synchronizedMap(value);
1905   }
1906
1907   /**
1908    * Wraps the specified set in {@link Collections#unmodifiableSet(Set)}.
1909    *
1910    * @param <E> The element type.
1911    * @param value The set to wrap.
1912    * @return The wrapped set.
1913    */
1914   @SuppressWarnings({
1915      "java:S1168"     // Pass-through null by design. Consider empty set.
1916   })
1917   public static <E> Set<E> synced(Set<E> value) {
1918      return value == null ? null : Collections.synchronizedSet(value);
1919   }
1920
1921   /**
1922    * Converts the specified collection to an array.
1923    *
1924    * <p>
1925    * Works on both object and primitive arrays.
1926    *
1927    * @param <E> The element type.
1928    * @param c The collection to convert to an array.
1929    * @param elementType The component type of the collection.
1930    * @return A new array.
1931    */
1932   public static <E> Object toArray(Collection<?> c, Class<E> elementType) {
1933      var a = Array.newInstance(elementType, c.size());
1934      Iterator<?> it = c.iterator();
1935      int i = 0;
1936      while (it.hasNext())
1937         Array.set(a, i++, it.next());
1938      return a;
1939   }
1940
1941   /**
1942    * Creates an {@link ArrayList} copy from a collection.
1943    *
1944    * @param <E> The element type.
1945    * @param value The collection to copy from.
1946    * @return A new modifiable list.
1947    */
1948   public static <E> List<E> toList(Collection<E> value) {
1949      return toList(value, false);
1950   }
1951
1952   /**
1953    * Creates an {@link ArrayList} copy from a collection.
1954    *
1955    * @param <E> The element type.
1956    * @param value The collection to copy from.
1957    * @param nullIfEmpty If <jk>true</jk> will return <jk>null</jk> if the collection is empty or null.
1958    * @return A new modifiable list, or an empty list if the input was <jk>null</jk> (when nullIfEmpty is false), or <jk>null</jk> if nullIfEmpty and the input was null or empty.
1959    */
1960   @SuppressWarnings({
1961      "java:S1168"     // Intentional null when nullIfEmpty and (null or empty).
1962   })
1963   public static <E> List<E> toList(Collection<E> value, boolean nullIfEmpty) {
1964      if (value == null)
1965         return nullIfEmpty ? null : new ArrayList<>();
1966      if (nullIfEmpty && value.isEmpty())
1967         return null;
1968      var l = new ArrayList<E>();
1969      value.forEach(l::add);
1970      return l;
1971   }
1972
1973   /**
1974    * Converts various collection-like objects to a {@link List}.
1975    *
1976    * <p>This utility method enables testing of any collection-like object by converting it to a List that can be
1977    * passed to methods such as TestUtils.assertList().</p>
1978    *
1979    * <h5 class='section'>Supported Input Types:</h5>
1980    * <ul>
1981    *    <li><b>List:</b> Returns the input unchanged</li>
1982    *    <li><b>Iterable:</b> Any collection, set, queue, etc. (converted to List preserving order)</li>
1983    *    <li><b>Iterator:</b> Converts iterator contents to List</li>
1984    *    <li><b>Enumeration:</b> Converts enumeration contents to List</li>
1985    *    <li><b>Stream:</b> Converts stream contents to List (stream is consumed)</li>
1986    *    <li><b>Map:</b> Converts map entries to List of Map.Entry objects</li>
1987    *    <li><b>Array:</b> Converts any array type (including primitive arrays) to List</li>
1988    * </ul>
1989    *
1990    * <h5 class='section'>Usage Examples:</h5>
1991    * <p class='bjava'>
1992    *    <jc>// Test a Set</jc>
1993    *    Set&lt;String&gt; <jv>mySet</jv> = Set.of(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>"c"</js>);
1994    *    assertList(toList(<jv>mySet</jv>), <js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>"c"</js>);
1995    *
1996    *    <jc>// Test an array</jc>
1997    *    String[] <jv>myArray</jv> = {<js>"x"</js>, <js>"y"</js>, <js>"z"</js>};
1998    *    assertList(toList(<jv>myArray</jv>), <js>"x"</js>, <js>"y"</js>, <js>"z"</js>);
1999    *
2000    *    <jc>// Test a primitive array</jc>
2001    *    <jk>int</jk>[] <jv>numbers</jv> = {1, 2, 3};
2002    *    assertList(toList(<jv>numbers</jv>), <js>"1"</js>, <js>"2"</js>, <js>"3"</js>);
2003    *
2004    *    <jc>// Test a Stream</jc>
2005    *    Stream&lt;String&gt; <jv>myStream</jv> = Stream.of(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>);
2006    *    assertList(toList(<jv>myStream</jv>), <js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>);
2007    *
2008    *    <jc>// Test a Map (converted to entries)</jc>
2009    *    Map&lt;String,Integer&gt; <jv>myMap</jv> = Map.of(<js>"a"</js>, 1, <js>"b"</js>, 2);
2010    *    assertList(toList(<jv>myMap</jv>), <js>"a=1"</js>, <js>"b=2"</js>);
2011    *
2012    *    <jc>// Test any Iterable collection</jc>
2013    *    Queue&lt;String&gt; <jv>myQueue</jv> = new LinkedList&lt;&gt;(List.of(<js>"first"</js>, <js>"second"</js>));
2014    *    assertList(toList(<jv>myQueue</jv>), <js>"first"</js>, <js>"second"</js>);
2015    * </p>
2016    *
2017    * <h5 class='section'>Integration with Testing:</h5>
2018    * <p>This method is specifically designed to work with testing frameworks to provide
2019    * a unified testing approach for all collection-like types. Instead of having separate assertion methods
2020    * for arrays, sets, and other collections, you can convert them all to Lists and use standard
2021    * list assertion methods.</p>
2022    *
2023    * @param o The object to convert to a List. Must not be null and must be a supported collection-like type.
2024    * @return A {@link List} containing the elements from the input object.
2025    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the input object cannot be converted to a List.
2026    * @see #arrayToList(Object)
2027    */
2028   @SuppressWarnings({
2029      "java:S1452"  // Wildcard required - List<?> for heterogeneous collections from various sources
2030   })
2031   public static final List<?> toList(Object o) {
2032      assertArgNotNull(ARG_o, o);
2033      if (o instanceof List<?> o2)
2034         return o2;
2035      if (o instanceof Iterable<?> o2)
2036         return StreamSupport.stream(o2.spliterator(), false).toList();
2037      if (o instanceof Iterator<?> o2)
2038         return StreamSupport.stream(Spliterators.spliteratorUnknownSize(o2, 0), false).toList();
2039      if (o instanceof Enumeration<?> o2)
2040         return Collections.list(o2);
2041      if (o instanceof Stream<?> o2)
2042         return o2.toList();
2043      if (o instanceof Map<?,?> o2)
2044         return toList(o2.entrySet());
2045      if (o instanceof Optional<?> o2)
2046         return o2.isEmpty() ? Collections.emptyList() : Collections.singletonList(o2.get());
2047      if (ClassUtils.isArray(o))
2048         return arrayToList(o);
2049      throw rex("Could not convert object of type {0} to a list", ClassUtils.className(o));
2050   }
2051
2052   /**
2053    * Converts the specified array to an <c>ArrayList</c>
2054    *
2055    * @param <E> The element type.
2056    * @param array The array to convert.
2057    * @param elementType
2058    *    The type of objects in the array.
2059    *    It must match the actual component type in the array.
2060    * @return A new {@link ArrayList}
2061    */
2062   @SuppressWarnings({
2063      "unchecked", // Parameter elementType is used for type inference, not runtime behavior
2064      "java:S1172", // Unused parameters kept for API consistency or framework requirements
2065   })
2066   public static <E> List<E> toList(Object array, Class<E> elementType) {
2067      var l = new ArrayList<E>(Array.getLength(array));
2068      for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(array); i++)
2069         l.add((E)Array.get(array, i));
2070      return l;
2071   }
2072
2073   /**
2074    * Recursively converts the specified array into a list of objects.
2075    *
2076    * @param array The array to convert.
2077    * @return A new {@link ArrayList}
2078    */
2079   public static List<Object> toObjectList(Object array) {
2080      var l = new ArrayList<>(Array.getLength(array));
2081      for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(array); i++) {
2082         var o = Array.get(array, i);
2083         if (ClassUtils.isArray(o))
2084            o = toObjectList(o);
2085         l.add(o);
2086      }
2087      return l;
2088   }
2089
2090   /**
2091    * Creates a new set from the specified collection.
2092    *
2093    * @param <E> The element type.
2094    * @param val The value to copy from.
2095    * @return A new {@link LinkedHashSet}, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was null.
2096    */
2097   public static <E> Set<E> toSet(Collection<E> val) {
2098      return val == null ? null : new LinkedHashSet<>(val);
2099   }
2100
2101   /**
2102    * Converts the specified array to a <c>Set</c>.
2103    *
2104    * <p>
2105    * The order of the entries in the set are the same as the array.
2106    *
2107    * @param <T> The entry type of the array.
2108    * @param array The array being wrapped in a <c>Set</c> interface.
2109    * @return The new set.
2110    */
2111   public static <T> Set<T> toSet(T[] array) {
2112      assertArgNotNull(ARG_array, array);
2113      return new AbstractSet<>() {
2114
2115         @Override /* Overridden from Set */
2116         public Iterator<T> iterator() {
2117            return new Iterator<>() {
2118               int i = 0;
2119
2120               @Override /* Overridden from Iterator */
2121               public boolean hasNext() {
2122                  return i < array.length;
2123               }
2124
2125               @Override /* Overridden from Iterator */
2126               public T next() {
2127                  if (i >= array.length)
2128                     throw new NoSuchElementException();
2129                  T t = array[i];
2130                  i++;
2131                  return t;
2132               }
2133
2134               @Override /* Overridden from Iterator */
2135               public void remove() {
2136                  throw uoex();
2137               }
2138            };
2139         }
2140
2141         @Override /* Overridden from Set */
2142         public int size() {
2143            return array.length;
2144         }
2145      };
2146   }
2147
2148   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2149   // Primitive array creation methods
2150   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2151
2152   /**
2153    * Creates a new {@link TreeSet} from the specified collection.
2154    *
2155    * @param <E> The element type.
2156    * @param value The value to copy from.
2157    * @return A new {@link TreeSet}, or an empty {@link TreeSet} if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
2158    */
2159   public static <E> SortedSet<E> toSortedSet(Collection<E> value) {
2160      if (value == null)
2161         return new TreeSet<>();
2162      var l = new TreeSet<E>();
2163      value.forEach(l::add);
2164      return l;
2165   }
2166
2167   /**
2168    * Creates a new {@link TreeSet} from the specified collection.
2169    *
2170    * @param <E> The element type.
2171    * @param value The value to copy from.
2172    * @param nullIfEmpty If <jk>true</jk> returns <jk>null</jk> if the collection is empty.
2173    * @return A new {@link TreeSet}, or an empty {@link TreeSet} if the input was <jk>null</jk>, or <jk>null</jk> if nullIfEmpty and the collection is empty.
2174    */
2175   @SuppressWarnings({
2176      "java:S1168"     // Intentional null when nullIfEmpty and empty.
2177   })
2178   public static <E> SortedSet<E> toSortedSet(Collection<E> value, boolean nullIfEmpty) {
2179      if (value == null)
2180         return new TreeSet<>();
2181      if (nullIfEmpty && value.isEmpty())
2182         return null;
2183      var l = new TreeSet<E>();
2184      value.forEach(l::add);
2185      return l;
2186   }
2187
2188   /**
2189    * Creates a new {@link TreeSet} containing a copy of the specified set.
2190    *
2191    * @param <T> The element type.
2192    * @param copyFrom The set to copy from.
2193    * @return A new {@link TreeSet}, or an empty {@link TreeSet} if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
2194    */
2195   public static <T> SortedSet<T> toSortedSet(Set<T> copyFrom) {
2196      return copyFrom == null ? new TreeSet<>() : new TreeSet<>(copyFrom);
2197   }
2198
2199   /**
2200    * Converts an array to a stream of objects.
2201    * @param array The array to convert.
2202    * @return A new stream.
2203    */
2204   public static Stream<Object> toStream(Object array) {
2205      assertArg(ClassUtils.isArray(array), "Arg was not an array.  Type: {0}", ClassUtils.className(array));
2206      var length = Array.getLength(array);
2207      return IntStream.range(0, length).mapToObj(i -> Array.get(array, i));
2208   }
2209
2210   /**
2211    * Converts the specified collection to an array of strings.
2212    *
2213    * <p>
2214    * Entries are converted to strings using {@link #toString()}.
2215    * <jk>null</jk> values remain <jk>null</jk>.
2216    *
2217    * @param c The collection to convert.
2218    * @return The collection as a string array.
2219    */
2220   public static String[] toStringArray(Collection<?> c) {
2221      var r = new String[c.size()];
2222      var i = 0;
2223      for (var o : c)
2224         r[i++] = stringify(o);
2225      return r;
2226   }
2227
2228   /**
2229    * Traverses all elements in the specified object and executes a consumer for each leaf.
2230    *
2231    * <p>
2232    * Input shapes are processed as follows:
2233    * <ul>
2234    *    <li><b>Flattened (recursively):</b> {@link Iterable}, {@link Stream}, and any array type (including all 8 primitive
2235    *       array types via {@link ClassUtils#isArray}), including arbitrary nesting and mixing of these.
2236    *    <li><b>Leaf (consumer called once):</b> anything else — {@link String}, {@link Map}, custom beans, boxed primitives, etc.
2237    *    <li><b>{@code null} handling:</b> a {@code null} root, or {@code null} encountered mid-traversal, causes the
2238    *       consumer to be called zero times — no {@code null} elements are ever delivered.
2239    * </ul>
2240    *
2241    * @param <T> The element type.
2242    * @param o The object to traverse.
2243    * @param c The consumer of the leaf elements.
2244    */
2245   public static <T> void traverse(Object o, Consumer<T> c) {
2246      CollectionUtils.<T>traverseToStream(o).forEach(c);
2247   }
2248
2249   /**
2250    * Shared recursive engine for {@link #traverse(Object, Consumer)}, {@link #accumulate(Object...)}, and
2251    * {@link #deepStream(Object...)}.
2252    *
2253    * <p>
2254    * Returns a lazily-evaluated {@link Stream} that recursively flattens {@link Iterable}, {@link Stream}, and
2255    * array inputs (including all primitive array types), treating everything else as a single leaf element.
2256    * {@code null} inputs return an empty stream.
2257    */
2258   @SuppressWarnings({
2259      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
2260   })
2261   private static <T> Stream<T> traverseToStream(Object o) {
2262      if (o == null)
2263         return Stream.empty();
2264      if (o instanceof Iterable<?> o2)
2265         return StreamSupport.stream(o2.spliterator(), false).flatMap(CollectionUtils::traverseToStream);
2266      if (o instanceof Stream<?> o2)
2267         return o2.flatMap(CollectionUtils::traverseToStream);
2268      if (ClassUtils.isArray(o))
2269         return toStream(o).flatMap(CollectionUtils::traverseToStream);
2270      return Stream.of((T)o);
2271   }
2272
2273   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2274   // Stream utilities
2275   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2276
2277   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2278   // Full-named factory/query methods (canonical forms; short-named methods delegate to these)
2279   //-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2280
2281   /**
2282    * Creates an array of objects (varargs form).
2283    *
2284    * @param <T> The component type of the array.
2285    * @param x The objects to place in the array.
2286    * @return A new array containing the specified objects.
2287    */
2288   @SafeVarargs
2289   public static <T> T[] array(T...x) {
2290      return x;
2291   }
2292
2293   /**
2294    * Creates a 2-dimensional array.
2295    *
2296    * @param <E> The element type of the inner arrays.
2297    * @param value The 1D arrays that will become rows in the 2D array.
2298    * @return A 2D array containing the specified rows.
2299    */
2300   @SafeVarargs
2301   public static <E> E[][] array2d(E[]...value) {
2302      return value;
2303   }
2304
2305   /**
2306    * Creates an Object array.
2307    *
2308    * @param value The objects to place in the array.
2309    * @return A new {@code Object[]} containing the specified objects.
2310    */
2311   public static Object[] objectArray(Object...value) {
2312      return value;
2313   }
2314
2315   /**
2316    * Returns null for the specified array type.
2317    *
2318    * <p>Intentional null return for assertion testing.
2319    *
2320    * @param <T> The component type.
2321    * @param type The component type class.
2322    * @return <jk>null</jk>.
2323    */
2324   @SuppressWarnings({
2325      "java:S1168",  // Intentional null return for assertion testing.
2326      "java:S1172"   // Parameter type is used for type inference, not runtime behavior
2327   })
2328   public static <T> T[] nullArray(Class<T> type) {
2329      return null;
2330   }
2331
2332   /**
2333    * Creates a LinkedList from the specified values.
2334    *
2335    * @param <T> The element type.
2336    * @param values The values to add to the list.
2337    * @return A new modifiable LinkedList containing the specified values.
2338    */
2339   @SafeVarargs
2340   public static <T> List<T> linkedList(T...values) {
2341      return new LinkedList<>(fixedSizeList(values));
2342   }
2343
2344   /**
2345    * Creates a HashSet from the specified values.
2346    *
2347    * @param <T> The element type.
2348    * @param values The values to add to the set.
2349    * @return A new modifiable HashSet containing the specified values.
2350    */
2351   @SafeVarargs
2352   public static <T> Set<T> hashSet(T...values) {
2353      return new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(values));
2354   }
2355
2356   /**
2357    * Creates an unmodifiable view of the specified list.
2358    *
2359    * @param <T> The element type.
2360    * @param value The list to make unmodifiable. Can be null.
2361    * @return An unmodifiable view of the list, or null if the input was null.
2362    */
2363   public static <T> List<T> unmodifiable(List<? extends T> value) {
2364      return value == null ? null : Collections.unmodifiableList(value);
2365   }
2366
2367   /**
2368    * Creates an unmodifiable view of the specified map.
2369    *
2370    * @param <K> The key type.
2371    * @param <V> The value type.
2372    * @param value The map to make unmodifiable. Can be null.
2373    * @return An unmodifiable view of the map, or null if the input was null.
2374    */
2375   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> unmodifiable(Map<? extends K,? extends V> value) {
2376      return value == null ? null : Collections.unmodifiableMap(value);
2377   }
2378
2379   /**
2380    * Creates an unmodifiable view of the specified set.
2381    *
2382    * @param <T> The element type.
2383    * @param value The set to make unmodifiable. Can be null.
2384    * @return An unmodifiable view of the set, or null if the input was null.
2385    */
2386   public static <T> Set<T> unmodifiable(Set<? extends T> value) {
2387      return value == null ? null : Collections.unmodifiableSet(value);
2388   }
2389
2390   /**
2391    * Creates an unmodifiable view of the specified sorted set.
2392    *
2393    * @param <T> The element type.
2394    * @param value The sorted set to make unmodifiable. Can be null.
2395    * @return An unmodifiable view of the sorted set, or null if the input was null.
2396    */
2397   public static <T> SortedSet<T> unmodifiable(SortedSet<T> value) {
2398      return value == null ? null : Collections.unmodifiableSortedSet(value);
2399   }
2400
2401   /**
2402    * Returns the element at the specified index, wrapped in an {@link Optional}.
2403    *
2404    * @param <E> The element type.
2405    * @param l The list. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
2406    * @param index The index to access.
2407    * @return An Optional containing the element, or an empty Optional if null/out-of-bounds.
2408    */
2409   public static <E> Optional<E> optionalAt(List<E> l, int index) {
2410      return Optional.ofNullable(elementAt(l, index));
2411   }
2412
2413   /**
2414    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the specified map is <jk>null</jk> or empty, otherwise returns the map.
2415    *
2416    * @param <K> The key type.
2417    * @param <V> The value type.
2418    * @param val The map to check.
2419    * @return <jk>null</jk> if the map is <jk>null</jk> or empty, otherwise the map itself.
2420    */
2421   @SuppressWarnings({ "java:S1168" // Intentional null return.
2422   })
2423   public static <K,V> Map<K,V> nullIfEmpty(Map<K,V> val) {
2424      return isEmpty(val) ? null : val;
2425   }
2426
2427   /**
2428    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the specified list is <jk>null</jk> or empty, otherwise returns the list.
2429    *
2430    * @param <E> The element type.
2431    * @param val The list to check.
2432    * @return <jk>null</jk> if the list is <jk>null</jk> or empty, otherwise the list itself.
2433    */
2434   @SuppressWarnings({ "java:S1168" // Intentional null return.
2435   })
2436   public static <E> List<E> nullIfEmpty(List<E> val) {
2437      return isEmpty(val) ? null : val;
2438   }
2439
2440   /**
2441    * Returns <jk>null</jk> if the specified set is <jk>null</jk> or empty, otherwise returns the set.
2442    *
2443    * @param <E> The element type.
2444    * @param val The set to check.
2445    * @return <jk>null</jk> if the set is <jk>null</jk> or empty, otherwise the set itself.
2446    */
2447   @SuppressWarnings({ "java:S1168" // Intentional null return.
2448   })
2449   public static <E> Set<E> nullIfEmpty(Set<E> val) {
2450      return isEmpty(val) ? null : val;
2451   }
2452
2453   /**
2454    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified collection is not null and not empty.
2455    *
2456    * @param c The collection to check.
2457    * @return <jk>true</jk> if not null and not empty.
2458    */
2459   public static boolean isNotEmpty(Collection<?> c) {
2460      return c != null && ! c.isEmpty();
2461   }
2462
2463   /**
2464    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified map is not null and not empty.
2465    *
2466    * @param m The map to check.
2467    * @return <jk>true</jk> if not null and not empty.
2468    */
2469   public static boolean isNotEmpty(Map<?,?> m) {
2470      return m != null && ! m.isEmpty();
2471   }
2472
2473   /**
2474    * Returns the element at the specified index.
2475    *
2476    * @param <E> The element type.
2477    * @param l The list. Can be null.
2478    * @param index The index to access.
2479    * @return The element, or null if out-of-bounds/null list.
2480    */
2481   @SuppressWarnings({ "java:S1168" // Intentional null return.
2482   })
2483   public static <E> E elementAt(List<E> l, int index) {
2484      if (l == null || index < 0 || index >= l.size())
2485         return null;
2486      return l.get(index);
2487   }
2488
2489   /**
2490    * Creates a new modifiable LinkedHashSet.
2491    *
2492    * @param <T> The element type.
2493    * @param values The values to add to the set.
2494    * @return A modifiable LinkedHashSet containing the specified values.
2495    */
2496   @SafeVarargs
2497   public static <T> Set<T> set(T...values) {
2498      assertArgNotNull(ARG_values, values);
2499      return new LinkedHashSet<>(Arrays.asList(values));
2500   }
2501
2502   /**
2503    * Creates an int array.
2504    *
2505    * @param value The values.
2506    * @return A new int array.
2507    */
2508   public static int[] intArray(int...value) { return value; }
2509
2510   /**
2511    * Creates a long array.
2512    *
2513    * @param value The values.
2514    * @return A new long array.
2515    */
2516   public static long[] longArray(long...value) { return value; }
2517
2518   /**
2519    * Creates a byte array from ints.
2520    *
2521    * @param value The int values to convert to bytes.
2522    * @return A new byte array.
2523    */
2524   public static byte[] byteArray(int...value) {
2525      var result = new byte[value.length];
2526      for (var i = 0; i < value.length; i++)
2527         result[i] = (byte)value[i];
2528      return result;
2529   }
2530
2531   /**
2532    * Creates a char array.
2533    *
2534    * @param value The values.
2535    * @return A new char array.
2536    */
2537   public static char[] charArray(char...value) { return value; }
2538
2539   /**
2540    * Creates a float array.
2541    *
2542    * @param value The values.
2543    * @return A new float array.
2544    */
2545   public static float[] floatArray(float...value) { return value; }
2546
2547   /**
2548    * Creates a double array.
2549    *
2550    * @param value The values.
2551    * @return A new double array.
2552    */
2553   public static double[] doubleArray(double...value) { return value; }
2554
2555   /**
2556    * Creates a boolean array.
2557    *
2558    * @param value The values.
2559    * @return A new boolean array.
2560    */
2561   public static boolean[] booleanArray(boolean...value) { return value; }
2562
2563   /**
2564    * Creates a short array from ints.
2565    *
2566    * @param value The int values to convert to shorts.
2567    * @return A new short array.
2568    */
2569   public static short[] shortArray(int...value) {
2570      var result = new short[value.length];
2571      for (var i = 0; i < value.length; i++)
2572         result[i] = (short)value[i];
2573      return result;
2574   }
2575
2576   private CollectionUtils() {}
2577}
